generators.label: Console command

files.content:
<?php
declare(strict_types=1);

namespace ${vendor}\${module}\Console\Command;

use Symfony\Component\Console\Command\Command;
use Symfony\Component\Console\Input\InputInterface;
use Symfony\Component\Console\Output\OutputInterface;

class ${commandName} extends Command
{
    protected function configure(): void
    {
        $this->setName('${cliName}')->setDescription('${commandName}');
        parent::configure();
    }

    protected function execute(InputInterface $input, OutputInterface $output): int
    {
        $output->writeln('<info>${cliName} ran</info>');
        return Command::SUCCESS;
    }
}

generators.postNote: Register in etc/di.xml under Magento\Framework\Console\CommandListComposite (or CommandList): add <item name="${cliName}" xsi:type="object">${vendor}\${module}\Console\Command\${commandName}</item>. (XML hand-merged.) Run: bin/magento ${cliName}.

generators.label: Controller action

files.content:
<?php
declare(strict_types=1);

namespace ${vendor}\${module}\Controller\${controller};

use Magento\Framework\App\Action\HttpGetActionInterface;
use Magento\Framework\Controller\ResultFactory;

class ${action} implements HttpGetActionInterface
{
    public function __construct(private readonly ResultFactory $resultFactory)
    {
    }

    public function execute()
    {
        return $this->resultFactory->create(ResultFactory::TYPE_PAGE);
    }
}

generators.postNote: Add a route in etc/frontend/routes.xml: <router id="standard"><route id="<frontName>" frontName="<frontName>"><module name="${vendor}_${module}"/></route></router>. (XML hand-merged.)

generators.label: Cron job

files.content:
<?php
declare(strict_types=1);

namespace ${vendor}\${module}\Cron;

class ${cronName}
{
    public function execute(): void
    {
        // scheduled work (${schedule})
    }
}

generators.postNote: Register in etc/crontab.xml: <group id="default"><job name="${vendor}_${module}_${cronName}" instance="${vendor}\${module}\Cron\${cronName}" method="execute"><schedule>${schedule}</schedule></job></group>. (XML hand-merged.)

generators.label: Declarative schema (db_schema.xml)
generators.postNote: Declarative schema over install/upgrade scripts (commerce/declarative-schema). The <table> is merged into an existing db_schema.xml (idempotent, deduped by table name); a new file is created with the schema root if absent. Apply: bin/magento setup:upgrade. Generate the whitelist: bin/magento setup:db-declaration:generate-whitelist --module-name=${vendor}_${module}.

generators.label: Transactional email template

files.content:
{{template config_path="design/email/header_template"}}

{{trans "Hello, %name" name=$data.name}}

{{trans "This is the ${templateLabel} email."}}

{{template config_path="design/email/footer_template"}}
<template id="${templateId}" label="${templateLabel}" file="${templateId}.html" type="html" module="${vendor}_${module}" area="frontend"/>

generators.postNote: Registered in etc/email_templates.xml (deduped by template id). Send it via the TransportBuilder using identifier "${templateId}". Edit the .html body + @vars as needed.

generators.label: GraphQL resolver

files.content:
<?php
declare(strict_types=1);

namespace ${vendor}\${module}\Model\Resolver;

use Magento\Framework\GraphQl\Config\Element\Field;
use Magento\Framework\GraphQl\Query\ResolverInterface;
use Magento\Framework\GraphQl\Schema\Type\ResolveInfo;

class ${resolver} implements ResolverInterface
{
    public function resolve(Field $field, $context, ResolveInfo $info, ?array $value = null, ?array $args = null)
    {
        // resolve ${queryField}
        return null;
    }
}

generators.postNote: schema.graphqls is create-only — if it already exists, add the field by hand: type Query { ${queryField}: String @resolver(class: "${vendor}\\${module}\\Model\\Resolver\\${resolver}") }

generators.label: Indexer

files.content:
<?php
declare(strict_types=1);

namespace ${vendor}\${module}\Model\Indexer;

use Magento\Framework\Indexer\ActionInterface;
use Magento\Framework\Mview\ActionInterface as MviewActionInterface;

class ${indexerName} implements ActionInterface, MviewActionInterface
{
    public function executeFull(): void
    {
        // rebuild the whole index
    }

    public function executeList(array $ids): void
    {
        // reindex the given ids
    }

    public function executeRow($id): void
    {
        // reindex a single id
    }

    public function execute($ids): void
    {
        $this->executeList((array) $ids);
    }
}

<indexer id="${indexerId}" view_id="${indexerId}" class="${vendor}\${module}\Model\Indexer\${indexerName}">
    <title translate="true">${indexerName}</title>
    <description translate="true">${indexerName} indexer</description>
</indexer>

<view id="${indexerId}" class="Magento\Framework\Mview\View" group="indexer">
    <subscriptions>
        <table name="${sourceTable}" entity_column="entity_id"/>
    </subscriptions>
</view>

generators.postNote: Registered in etc/indexer.xml + etc/mview.xml (deduped by id). Reindex: bin/magento indexer:reindex ${indexerId}. Implement the executeFull/executeList/executeRow bodies.

generators.label: Module (Vendor_Module)

files.content:
<?php
declare(strict_types=1);

use Magento\Framework\Component\ComponentRegistrar;

ComponentRegistrar::register(
    ComponentRegistrar::MODULE,
    '${vendor}_${module}',
    __DIR__
);

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<config xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
        xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="urn:magento:framework:Module/etc/module.xsd">
    <module name="${vendor}_${module}"/>
</config>

generators.postNote: Enable with: bin/magento module:enable ${vendor}_${module} && bin/magento setup:upgrade. Then generate components (plugin/observer/controller/...) into this module.

generators.label: Message-queue consumer

files.content:
<?php
declare(strict_types=1);

namespace ${vendor}\${module}\Model\Queue;

class ${handlerName}
{
    /**
     * @param mixed $message
     */
    public function process($message): void
    {
        // handle the dequeued $message
    }
}
<consumer name="${consumerName}" queue="${queueName}" connection="db" consumerInstance="Magento\Framework\MessageQueue\Consumer" handler="${vendor}\${module}\Model\Queue\${handlerName}::process"/>

generators.postNote: Consumer registered in etc/queue_consumer.xml (deduped by name). For a full publish/subscribe also add etc/communication.xml, etc/queue_topology.xml + etc/queue_publisher.xml (coordinated topic/exchange values — hand-author). Run: bin/magento queue:consumers:start ${consumerName}.

generators.label: Event observer

files.content:
<?php
declare(strict_types=1);

namespace ${vendor}\${module}\Observer;

use Magento\Framework\Event\Observer;
use Magento\Framework\Event\ObserverInterface;

class ${observerName} implements ObserverInterface
{
    public function execute(Observer $observer): void
    {
        // $event = $observer->getEvent(); handle ${event}
    }
}

<event name="${event}">
    <observer name="${vendor}_${module}_${observerName}" instance="${vendor}\${module}\Observer\${observerName}"/>
</event>

generators.postNote: Registered in etc/events.xml automatically (an <event name="${event}"> with your <observer>). NOTE: for MULTIPLE observers on the SAME event, add the extra <observer> under the existing <event> by hand (the merge dedups by event name). Use etc/<area>/events.xml for area-specific events.

generators.label: Plugin (interceptor)

files.content:
<?php
declare(strict_types=1);

namespace ${vendor}\${module}\Plugin;

class ${pluginName}
{
    // before / around / after — implement the one(s) you need.
    // public function beforeMethodName($subject, $arg) { return [$arg]; }
    // public function afterMethodName($subject, $result) { return $result; }
}

<type name="${targetClass}">
    <plugin name="${vendor}_${module}_${pluginName}" type="${vendor}\${module}\Plugin\${pluginName}" sortOrder="10"/>
</type>

generators.postNote: Registered in etc/di.xml automatically (a <type name="${targetClass}"> with your <plugin>). NOTE: for MULTIPLE plugins on the SAME target class, add the extra <plugin> under the existing <type> by hand (the merge dedups by target name). sortOrder when conflicting (commerce/plugin-sort-order).

generators.label: REST API endpoint

files.content:
<?php
declare(strict_types=1);

namespace ${vendor}\${module}\Api;

interface ${resourceName}RepositoryInterface
{
    /**
     * @return mixed[]
     */
    public function getList(): array;
}

<?php
declare(strict_types=1);

namespace ${vendor}\${module}\Model;

use ${vendor}\${module}\Api\${resourceName}RepositoryInterface;

class ${resourceName}Repository implements ${resourceName}RepositoryInterface
{
    public function getList(): array
    {
        return [];
    }
}

<route url="${url}" method="${httpMethod}">
    <service class="${vendor}\${module}\Api\${resourceName}RepositoryInterface" method="getList"/>
    <resources>
        <resource ref="anonymous"/>
    </resources>
</route>
<preference for="${vendor}\${module}\Api\${resourceName}RepositoryInterface" type="${vendor}\${module}\Model\${resourceName}Repository"/>

generators.postNote: Route registered in etc/webapi.xml + the interface->model preference in etc/di.xml. NOTE: webapi.xml dedups by url only, so two methods on the SAME url need the second <route> by hand. The route is open (anonymous); tighten <resources> for auth. Implement getList().

generators.label: Admin UI listing component

files.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<listing xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
         xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="urn:magento:module:Magento_Ui:etc/ui_configuration.xsd">
    <argument name="data" xsi:type="array">
        <item name="js_config" xsi:type="array">
            <item name="provider" xsi:type="string">${componentName}.${componentName}_data_source</item>
        </item>
    </argument>
    <settings>
        <spinner>${componentName}_columns</spinner>
    </settings>
    <dataSource name="${componentName}_data_source" component="Magento_Ui/js/grid/provider">
        <settings>
            <updateUrl path="mui/index/render"/>
        </settings>
        <aclResource>${vendor}_${module}::management</aclResource>
        <dataProvider class="Magento\Framework\View\Element\UiComponent\DataProvider\DataProvider" name="${componentName}_data_source">
            <settings>
                <requestFieldName>entity_id</requestFieldName>
                <primaryFieldName>entity_id</primaryFieldName>
            </settings>
        </dataProvider>
    </dataSource>
    <columns name="${componentName}_columns">
        <column name="entity_id">
            <settings>
                <label translate="true">ID</label>
            </settings>
        </column>
        <column name="title">
            <settings>
                <label translate="true">Title</label>
            </settings>
        </column>
    </columns>
</listing>

generators.postNote: Listing UI component created (create-only). Wire a grid collection in etc/di.xml: a <virtualType ... type="Magento\Framework\View\Element\UiComponent\DataProvider\CollectionFactory"> with an item for "${componentName}_data_source" mapped to your resource-model Collection (hand-merge). Render it from a controller/layout.

generators.label: Frontend widget

files.content:
<?php
declare(strict_types=1);

namespace ${vendor}\${module}\Block\Widget;

use Magento\Framework\View\Element\Template;
use Magento\Widget\Block\BlockInterface;

class ${widgetName} extends Template implements BlockInterface
{
    protected $_template = '${vendor}_${module}::widget/${widgetName}.phtml';
}
escapeHtml($block->getData('title')) ?>

<widget id="${vendor}_${module}_${widgetName}" class="${vendor}\${module}\Block\Widget\${widgetName}">
    <label>${widgetName}</label>
    <description>${widgetName} widget</description>
    <parameters>
        <parameter name="title" xsi:type="text" visible="true" sort_order="10">
            <label>Title</label>
        </parameter>
    </parameters>
</widget>

generators.postNote: Registered in etc/widget.xml (deduped by widget id). The widget is now selectable in Admin > Content > Widgets. Add more <parameter> entries in widget.xml as needed.
